IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. 2026-03-11T11:44:19.2405964Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T11:42:00.873453069Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":2,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). 2026-03-11T11:44:19.2623961Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T11:43:57.402754663Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a bug in the `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` generator where PUT operation body parameters are emitted as optional (`data = null` / `data = default`) instead of required. This affects all `CreateOrUpdate`/`Update` methods generated via TypeSpec MPG migration, and has also introduced a **behavioral regression on the already-shipped** `ConfluentOrganizationCollection.CreateOrUpdate` API (was required in v1.2.1, is now optional).\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Issue Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Type:** Bug / Design Regression in code generator\n- **Component:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` — the management plane TypeSpec generator\n- **Severity:** Medium-High — it's a behavioral regression on a shipped API surface and violates ARM SDK conventions for all newly migrated packages\n- **Related PR:** #54889 (Confluent MPG migration, where the bug was first observed)\n- **Affected methods (examples):**\n  - `ConnectorCollection.CreateOrUpdate(WaitUntil, string, ConnectorData data = null, ...)`\n  - `SCClusterRecordCollection.CreateOrUpdate(WaitUntil, string, SCClusterRecordData data = null, ...)`\n  - `ConfluentOrganizationCollection.CreateOrUpdate(...)` — regression from shipped v1.2.1\n- **Convention:** All existing management plane SDKs emit PUT body params as required; this generator should match that convention.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Debugging Strategy\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Locate body-parameter generation logic** in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`:\n   - Look for where method signatures for `CreateOrUpdate` are emitted.\n   - Check where the generator decides if a parameter is optional vs required — likely in the emitter TypeScript code (`emitter/src/`) or in the C# generator (`generator/`).\n2. **Check TypeSpec input:** Confirm that in the TypeSpec spec(s) the PUT body is marked required (no `?`). If it's optional in the spec, a spec fix may also be needed, but the convention for ARM PUT bodies is that they should always be required.\n3. **Compare with correct output:** Look at an existing SDK generated via the old AutoRest pipeline (e.g., `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute`) to confirm expected signature shape.\n4. **Search for regression:** Run a broad grep across all files recently generated by the mgmt generator for `Data data = null` or `Data data = default` to gauge the blast radius of this issue beyond Confluent.\n\n```bash\ngrep -r \"Data data = null\\|Data data = default\" sdk/*/Azure.ResourceManager.*/src/Generated/\n```\n\n5. **Check emitter options/visitor logic:** There may be a visitor or transform step that unconditionally marks body params as optional — inspect `FlattenPropertyVisitor` or any operation visitor in the generator.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Suggested Sub-tasks / Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Identify the specific emitter/generator location that makes PUT body params optional\n- [ ] Fix the generator to emit PUT body params as required (no default value)\n- [ ] Regenerate the Confluent SDK (PR #54889) with the fixed generator\n- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to validate fix across test projects\n- [ ] Audit other in-flight MPG migrations (if any) that may be affected by the same issue\n- [ ] Assess API compatibility impact for `ConfluentOrganizationCollection.CreateOrUpdate` — since v1.2.1 had it required, making it optional is a (non-breaking, but confusing) change; consider whether to explicitly document this or revert in the shipped package\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- Generator code: [`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)\n- CODEOWNERS for this area: `@JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang`\n- Related PR (Confluent migration): #54889\n- ARM SDK conventions: [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET — Management Plane](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n","item_number":56966},"name":"add_comment"}}}
